titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Meserve-Kunhardt Family papers
titlestmt.author: Gladys Garcia and Michael L. Forstrom

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 8, 2025 at 23:07

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: 191 boxes + 8 glass plate boxes, 4 broadside, 4 art, 1 record album storage

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 14 (record album storage) and Box 113 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. For further information consult Access Services.
Boxes 164-167, 169, and 200-205: in-process material pending conservation treatment. For further information consult Access Services.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from MKF Funding, Llc, Peter W. Kunhardt and Philip B. Kunhardt, III, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and with a gift from the Rice Family Foundation, 2015.
Gift of Peter W. Kunhardt and Philip B. Kunhardt, III, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Frederick Hill Meserve Papers, 1835-1962. II. Dorothy Kunhardt Papers, 1862-2008. III. Family Papers, 1806-1998. IV. Family Photographs, 1856-1994. V. Research Materials, 1726-2008. VI. Computer media, 1989-1990.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Frederick Hill Meserve (1865-1962) began collecting photographs around 1897-1900 to illustrate the American Civil War memoir of his father, William Neal Meserve (1840-1928) with images of persons and sites mentioned therein. The elder Meserve drafted his narrative during the 1890s; it covers his military service from August 1862 to June 1865 and includes accounts of battles at Antietam (where he reportedly suffered two wounds), Fredericksburg, the Wilderness, and Cold Harbor, as well as engagements outside Petersburg, including the battles at the Crater and Weldon Railroad.

Over a period of 60 years as he amassed an extensive collection of photographs documenting the American Civil War as well as American history and culture over the long nineteenth century, Frederick Hill Meserve became the preeminent collector and historian of photographic images of Abraham Lincoln. He sought to identify and preserve every photograph taken of the sixteenth president.

Meserve privately published many illustrated works, including his twenty-eight-volume Historical Portraits: A Part of the Collection of Americana of Frederick Hill Meserve, New York City (New York: Privately printed, 1913-1915).

Eventually, Meserve's daughter, Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt (1901-1979), joined his research and collecting efforts. Her son, Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr. (1928-2006) continued the project, writing extensively about Lincoln and preserving what became known as the Meserve-Kunhardt Collection at his home in Chappaqua, New York. Together, Dorothy and Philip, Jr. co-wrote Mathew Brady and His World (Morristown,Time-Life Books, 1977) and Twenty Days (New York, Harper & Row, 1965).

In 1981, the National Portrait Gallery acquired part of the Meserve Collection from the family. That collection includes the "cracked-plate" portrait of Abraham Lincoln by Alexander Gardner and more than 5,400 original negatives from the studio of Mathew B. Brady.

The descendants of Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr., including his sons Philip Bradish Kunhardt, III (born 1951), and Peter Whiting Kunhardt, Sr. (born 1952), as well as his grandson, Peter Whiting Kunhardt, Jr. (born 1982), continued to care for the remaining collection. In 2002, they formed the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation, a tax-exempt nonprofit organization dedicated to the preservation of oral and visual history and its use to inform, educate, and inspire.

In 2004, the National Endowment for the Humanities awarded the Meserve-Kunhardt Collection a Save America's Treasures grant, declaring that "the collection is an essential tool for Lincoln scholarship and for studying American cities and social movements." With this grant and other funding, the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation sought to preserve the collection and make it available to the public.

In 2007, the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation established a new division, the Gordon Parks Foundation, dedicated to preserving the creative work of photographer, author, and film director Gordon Parks (1912-2006). The foundation subsequently acquired the archive of another photographer, Ed Clark (1911-2000).

In summer 2009, the collections administered by the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation relocated to the State University of New York-Purchase. Shortly before acquisition of the Meserve-Kunhardt Collection by the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation relocated its collections to Pleasantville, New York. Material related to Gordon Parks and Ed Clark is not part of the collection acquired by the Beinecke.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Meserve-Kunhardt Family Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection covers over five generations of Meserve-Kunhardt family history and their historical interest in Abraham Lincoln and the long 19th century. The collection spans from 1726 to 2008. The bulk of the material dating between 1890 and 1969 was collected by Frederick Hill Meserve and Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt. The collection covers the military career of William Neal Meserve, research of Frederick Hill Meserve and his daughter Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt together, research by Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt and her son Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr., and recent research contributions of Philip Bradish Kunhardt, III. Other topics covered include Meserve-Kunhardt family genealogical research, relationships with extended family members, the research career of Frederick Hill Meserve, children's book career of Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt, Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr.'s research and publishing, development of the Meserve-Kunhardt collection, women in the workforce during the great depression, and Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt's experiences attending Bryn Mawr College in the 1920's.

The collection consists chiefly of correspondence, manuscript materials, periodicals, various photographic formats, and ephemera. Meserve-Kunhardt Collection (MES) numbers and alternate numbering systems used by the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation are present throughout the collection, mostly among the photographic materials. See Series IV. Family photographs for more information. Books originally part of the Meserve-Kunhardt Collection and Meserve-Kunhardt Family papers separated from collection and processed as separate titles.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Meserve-Kunhardt Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Nitrate film negatives, boxes 204-205, were removed from the collection. Film negatives destroyed after digitization due to their condition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into four subseries: Correspondence, Writings, Financial and Legal Records, and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Frederick Hill Meserve papers in Series I. include correspondence, drafts and printed versions of writings, financial and legal records, and personal papers relating to businessman and collector Frederick Hill Meserve (1866-1962). The papers document Meserve's personal and professional interests and activities, including research into Abraham Lincoln and construction of the Meserve-Kunhardt collection (GEN MSS 1430).

Correspondence in the series chiefly consists of incoming letters to Frederick Hill Meserve. Drafts and carbon copies of letters from Meserve to others, documented in folder notes, can be found scattered throughout the subseries. Correspondence dates from the late 1880s, when Meserve was a student at M.I.T., to his death in 1962, and addresses personal, family, business, and research matters. There are letters from members of the Buckley, Buzell, French, Hill, Kunhardt, Meserve, Milliken, Shackford, and Turner families, among others. The largest correspondence file consists of circa 275 letters from Meserve's wife Edith (Turner) Meserve.

The bulk of Frederick Hill Meserve's outgoing correspondence is in Series II. Dorothy Kunhardt papers and Series III. Family papers. Additional material concerning members of the Meserve and related families can be found in Series III. Family Papers and Series IV. Family photographs. Additional correspondence and research materials on Lincoln, the Civil War, the Meserve-Kunhardt collection, and the Meserve and related families are in Series V. Research materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 14 (Record album storage): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. For further information, consult Access Services.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is organized into six subseries: Family Correspondence, General Correspondence, Writings, Personal Papers, Kunhardt Family Childhood Works, and Meserve Kunhardt Foundation Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes the professional and personal papers of Dorothy Kunhardt, documenting her work as an Abraham Lincoln historian and children's book author. It features research files and manuscript drafts solely written by her, her and her son, Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr., for books such as Mathew Brady and His World and Twenty Days , as well as writings solely by Philip, Jr. The series contains incoming correspondence from colleagues,and family members, such as her father Frederick Hill Meserve, research partner Josephine Cobb, and other third-party correspondence. Most general and mixed correspondence is related to her research and work on Abraham Lincoln, the long 19th century, and with rare book traders. Also included are materials related to the development of the Meserve-Kunhardt collection and Foundation, Dorothy's life as a student at Bryn Mawr College, and early childhood works by her children Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr., Kenneth Bradish Kunhardt, Edith Kunhardt Davis, and Nancy Kunhardt Lodge. More material related to the Kunhardts can be found in Series III. Family papers, and Series IV. Family photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials arranged alphabetically and chronologically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into four subseries: Meserve Family Papers, Kunhardt Family Papers, Comstock and Kellogg Family Papers, and Buzell Family Papers.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Series III. consolidates the papers of the Meserve, Kunhardt, and other related families. The papers provide a rich resource into the personal and professional activities of these families with ties to the Eastern United States over multiple generations dating from the 1700s.
Key components of family trees and links between families:
Leighton and Hill family: Andrew Leighton (1767-1826) and Margaret Babb (1772-1848)
-Sallie Leighton (1793-1839) and Andrew Neal Hill (1787-1858)
--Levi Gerrish Hill (1812-1898) and Abigail Burnham Shackford (1814-1895)
---Clara Ann (Hill) French (1839-1928)
---Abby Augusta (Hill) Meserve (1842-1912)
---Adelaide Shackford (Hill) Buckley (1846-1910)
---Margaret Leighton (Hill) Milliken (1851-1882)
The Meserve and Hill families are related through the marriage of William Neal Meserve (1840-1928) and Abby Augusta Hill (1842-1912), both originally of Strafford County, New Hampshire.
Meserve family: Isaac Hall Meserve (1813-1875) and Martha Waterhouse (1815-1847)
-William Neal Meserve (1840-1928) and Abby Augusta Hill (1842-1912)
--Frederick Hill Meserve (1866-1962) and Edith Turner (1890-1965)
---Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt (1901-1979)
---Helen Meserve (1904-1986)
---Frederick Leighton Meserve (1907-1968?)
Kunhardt family: George E. Kunhardt (1861-1935) and Martha E. Knapp (1864-1944)
-Philip Bradish Kunhardt (1900-1963) and Dorothy Meserve (1901-1979)
--Nancy (Kunhardt) Lodge (1927-1997)
--Philip B. Kunhardt, Jr. (1928-2006)
--Kenneth B. Kunhardt (1930-1995)
--Edith T. (Kunhardt) Davis (1937-2020)

The Meserve and Kunhardt families are related through the marriage of Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt (1901-1979), the eldest child of Frederick Hill Meserve and Edith (Turner) Meserve, and Philip Bradish Kunhardt (1900-1963), originally of North Andover, Massachusetts.
Comstock and Kellogg family: Peter Comstock (1796-1874) and Lucy Jackson (1791-1836)
-Laura D. (Comstock) Baker (1819-1877)
-Demmis Dewey (Comstock) Kellogg (1821-1900)
-Allen Comstock (1824-1900)
-Evaline Augusta (Comstock) White (1829-1897)
Peter Comstock (1796-1874) and Eliza Brewster (1811-1876)
-Mary Eliza Comstock (1841-1924)
Demmis Dewey Comstock (1821-1900) and Charles White Kellogg (1815-1896)
-Peter C. Kellogg (1841-1905)
-Gertrude Kellogg (1843-1903)
-Fanny Kellogg (1848-1948)
The Comstock and Kellogg families are related through the marriage of Demmis Dewey Comstock (1821-1900) and Charles White Kellogg (1815-1896).

The Meserve and Kellogg families are related through the marriage of Frederick Leighton Meserve (1907-1968), the youngest child of Frederick Hill Meserve and Edith (Turner) Meserve, and Kathleen Kellogg (1906-1999), the daughter of Charles S. Kellogg (1870-1936) and Ida May Freeman (1874-1962).
Buzell family: Solomon Buzell (1761-1813) and Susannah Clark (1776-1857)
-Solomon Clark Buzell (1806-1882) and Margaret A. Harvey (1815-1888)
-Samuel B. Buzell (1801-1853) and Matilda E. Prentice (1802-1875)
--George Buzell (1834-1893)
--Susan M. Buzell (1836-1927)
Solomon Buzell (1761-1812/3) and Elizabeth Burnham (1759-1797)
-Nancy Burnham Buzell (1787-1873) and Samuel Shackford (1782-1832)
--Abigail Burnham Shackford (1814-1895) and Levi Gerrish Hill (1812-1898)
---Abby Augusta (Hill) Meserve (1842-1912)
The Meserve and Buzell families are related through the marriages and descendants of Solomon Buzell (1761-1813), originally of Northwood, New Hampshire.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subdivisions: Correspondence, Writings, Financial and Legal Records, Printed Materials, and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Meserve family papers in Series III. contains correspondence, writings, financial and legal records, printed materials, and personal papers dating from 1826 to 1959. Materials span six generations among members of the Meserve and related Buckley, Hill, Leighton, Milliken, Shackford, and Turner families.

The subseries features the papers of Frederick Hill Meserve's mother, Abby A. (Hill) Meserve (1842-1912). In addition to correspondence and drafts for several short writings, there are financial records from Dover, New Hampshire, Grand Junction, Colorado, and other locations dating from 1887 to 1912. Invoices and receipts, along with account registers, document expenses for personal and household goods and services, including books and newspapers, stationery supplies, clothing, coal, furniture and carpeting, groceries, hardware and kitchen supplies, and magazine subscriptions, as well as bookbinding, dentistry, photography, plumbing, tailoring, upholstery, and utilities.

Records relating to the estate of Abby H. Meserve's father, Levi Gerrish Hill (1812-1898), include additional invoices and receipts, as well as deeds documenting property transactions in Dover, Strafford County, New Hampshire.

The earliest document among the Meserve family papers is the last will and testament of Andrew Leighton (1767-1826), a great-great-grandfather of Frederick Hill Meserve. The will dates from 1826 and identifies Leighton as a resident of Strafford County, New Hampshire.

The bulk of Frederick Hill Meserve's outgoing correspondence can be found in Series III. There is a large file of circa 580-600 letters from Meserve to his wife Edith (Turner) Meserve dating from 1904 to 1922, in the first two decades of their marriage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subdivisions: Correspondence, Writings of Philip B. Kunhardt, Jr., and Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kunhardt family papers consist of correspondence and other material dating from 1835 to 1998. Materials span four to five generations among members of the Kunhardt and related Meserve and Lodge families.

The largest component of the Kunhardt family papers is the correspondence of Philip B. Kunhardt, Jr. (1928-2006), one of the Kunhardt family custodians of the Meserve-Kunhardt collection. The two largest correspondence files, with Time-Life Books and a legal firm, concern Matthew Brady and his world (Time-Life Books, 1977) and the estate of his mother Dorothy Kunhardt. Matthew Brady and his world , co-authored by Philip B. Kunhardt, Jr. and Dorothy Kunhardt, is based on Brady photographs in the Meserve-Kunhardt Collection. Other materials related to the work, such as research notes and drafts, are in Series II. Dorothy Kunhardt papers.

The earliest document among the Kunhardt family papers is a piece of printed ephemera celebrating the silver anniversary of Carl Philipp Kunhardt and his wife Henriette (Geffecken) Kunhardt in 1835. There is also an autograph book attributed to Carl Philipp Kunhardt with entries by various members of the Kunhardt, Geffecken, and other families of Lubeck and Hamburg, Germany. Entries in the autograph book, dating from 1850 to 1851, are in German.

did.unittitle: Copy of letter from Kunhardt to Ms. Abrams removed from Adrian Malone Productions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Philip to Edith enclosing 62 (5.5 x 8 cm) black-and-white photographic prints of historical figures, some identified as part of the "Meserve collection"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subdivisions: Correspondence, Financial and Legal Records, Gertrude Kellogg Papers, and Other Papers.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Kellogg Family Papers (MS 341), New York University Special Collections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Comstock and Kellogg family papers contain correspondence, financial and legal records, and assorted personal papers dating from 1837 to 1926. Materials appear to span five generations among members of the Comstock, Kellogg, and related Baker, Snow, and White families.

The bulk of the Comstock and Kellogg family papers relate to Charles White Kellogg (1815-1896) and Demmis Dewey (Comstock) Kellogg (1821-1900), both of New York, and their three children, including actress and performer Gertrude Kellogg. Correspondence and financial records document the couple's personal and business activities during the middle decades of the 19th century. Invoices and receipts document expenses for personal and household goods and services, including groceries, coal, wood, clothing, jewelry, furniture, and appliances, as well as plumbing, tailoring, carpentry, dentistry, upholstery, painting, and music and dance instruction.

The Gertrude Kellogg correspondence includes letters from American and English authors and actors and others connected to the theatrical stage. Prominent correspondents include Wilkie Collins, Georgina Hogarth, and members of the Dickens family.

Printed materials in the subseries can be found for Fuller & Kellogg and C. W. Kellogg & Co. (both produce commission merchants), for Kellogg & Co. (oil merchants), and for Peter C. Kellogg & Co. (auction and commission dealers in fashionably bred horses and cattle).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subdivisions: Correspondence and Other Papers.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Variations on the family name. including Buzel, Buzell, Buzelle, and Buzzell, can be found in different sources. Buzell appears on various legal documents for Susan M. (1836-1927), while Buzelle appears the preferred usage for Susan and her brother George (1834-1893) in the federal census and in correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Buzell family papers contain correspondence, financial and legal records, and assorted personal papers dating from 1806 to 1919. Materials span three generations among members of the Buzell and related Harvey, Milliken, and Prentice families.

The papers consist chiefly of correspondence for George B. (1834-1893) and Suzan M. Buzell (1836-1927) dating from the 1880s and 1890s. Other papers include a commonplace book, dating from 1838, from their uncle Solomon Clark Buzell (1806-1882) and numerous deeds for Solomon Clark Buzell and his wife Margaret A. (Harvey) Buzell documenting property transactions in Exeter, Rockingham County, New Hampshire. The earliest record among the Buzell family papers is a deed, dating from 1806, documenting the acquisition of property in Northwood, New Hampshire by Margaret's father, Judge John Harvey (1774-1849).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes autograph letter fragment to "Mrs. Creighton," possibly in hand of Susan Buzelle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes third-party letter

did.unittitle: Campbell, P. to Brooklyn Bureau of Charities
Autograph notes, possibly in the hand of George B. Buzell
"Circular" relating to The Exeter Broken Bones and Tumble-down Club
Commonplace book of S. C. Buzell
Portland Savings Bank account book of Susan M. Buzelle

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 200-205: in-process material pending conservation treatment. For further information, consult Access Services.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is organized into five subseries: Gelatin and Albumen Prints, Glass Plate Negatives, Cased Photographs, Other Formats. and Film Negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains mostly photographs of the Meserve, Kunhardt, Turner, Shackford, Smythe, and Pullman families, as well as distant relatives of these families. Photographic formats include gelatin and albumen prints, ambrotypes, tintypes, platinum prints, glass plate negatives, film negatives, safety film, daguerreotype photographs, photograph albums, cyanotypes, digital photos, and a hand-painted ceramic plate. Glass plate negatives are subdivided into large, medium, small, and other assorted sizes. Most photographs were taken in the mid-nineteenth or early twentieth century. The series also contains portraits, landscapes, family vacations in the United States and internationally, residences, and group photos. Meserve (MES) numbers that were originally created by Frederick Hill Meserve and continued by the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation, that consist of five digits, are found throughout series. Three digit numbers or numbers including an alphabetical letter, belong to an alternate numbering system used by the Meserve-Kunhardt Foundation, are also found through out the series. More material and family trees explaining connections across the Meserve, Kunhardt, Turner, Shackford, Smythe, and other extended families can be found in Series III: Family papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 163 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. For further information, consult Access Services.
Boxes 164-167 and 169: in-process material pending conservation treatment. For further information, consult Access Services.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is organized into four subseries: Historical Files, Photographs, Printed Materials, and Geneaological Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first three subseries in Series V. consist of materials relating chiefly to research into Abraham Lincoln and his family, the Civil War, Matthew Brady and photography, and construction and service of the Meserve-Kunhardt collection.

The Historical research files are subdivided into chronological and subject files. The Chronological file includes correspondence, manuscript notes and drafts, photocopies, and printed materials. Correspondence in the chronological file dates from 1903 to 2006 and spans at least three generations of Meserve-Kunhardt Collection custodians, from Frederick Hill Meserve to Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt and then to Philip B. Kunhardt Jr. The bulk of the correspondence consists of original letters to Frederick Hill Meserve and copies of his outgoing letters to others. There are also "internal" letters from Meserve to his daughter Dorothy. Printed materials, which probably make up the largest component of the chronological file, include clippings, full issues of journals and periodicals, such as Lincoln Herald and Lincoln Lore , book and manuscript catalogs, reprints and offprints, and other ephemera.

The Subject file preserves similar types of material as the chronological file, but the subdivision is arranged alphabetically by name and/or heading. Manuscript notes in the hand of Dorothy (Meserve) Kunhardt are common within these files.

The Photographs subseries contains materials used by Dorthy Meserve Kunhardt and her son Philip Bradish Kunhardt, Jr. in their historical research and writing related to Abraham Lincoln and the long nineteeth century. The most common photographic formats are albumen, silver gelatin, film negatives, copy photographs, and photograph albums. Materials are organized alphabetically and by format.

The Printed materials subseries consists of previously unsorted journals, newspapers, clippings, book and manuscript catalogs, and other ephemera. Materials are subdivided for periodicals and other material and arranged alphabetically by title and type of material respectively.

The Genealogical files represent the efforts of Frederick Hill Meserve and others to compile information on the Meserve and related families. Materials are arranged alphabetically first by family name, with research notes, biographical portraits and sketches, charts, clippings and printed ephemera, and, in some cases, personal documents such as deeds and wills. Packets of mixed material have been preserved at the end of the subseries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 157: Restricted fragile papers. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Meserve Lincoln numbers 98-99

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically and by format therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital file and print out of William Morris Smith's photo, dedication of the battle monument. Photo originally taken on June 10, 1865 in Bull Run, Virginia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in series contain computer media containing thank you notes, journal entries, and a QuickBooks file relating to a boat owned by the Kunhardt family.

did.physdesc: 737.28 Kilobytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
